code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test13-JAN-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or advanced kidney disease. No evidence of urinary tract infection (negative nitrite, leukocyte esterase, and hemoglobin). Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of renal impairment.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
